title: Podman build multistage does not use local image
body:
Original issue: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27197

I can't re-open issue and seems no notifications, so I create another one.

```
❯ podman --version
podman version 5.7.0

❯ podman info
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.42.1
...
```

and issue still here. I can't re-open issue b/o permissions.

REPRODUCING:
1. put the repro.sh script into an empty directory
2. adjust registry to some really existing registry you have read/write access
3. run script - it fails

repro.sh:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

# We need write access to this registry to reproduce
REGISTRY=myregistry.corp.local/bug

echo "Generate containers for multistage"
for name in one two three; do
    cat <<EOF > "Containerfile.$name"
FROM debian:trixie-20251117
EOF
    podman build -f "Containerfile.$name" -t "$REGISTRY/fake-image-$name:latest" .
    podman push "$REGISTRY/fake-image-$name:latest"
done

echo "List images"
podman image ls "$REGISTRY/fake-image*"

echo "Change containers locally: add file generation"
for name in one two three; do
    cat <<EOF >> "Containerfile.$name"
RUN echo "name $name" > /$name
EOF
    echo "build, but not push"
    podman build -f "Containerfile.$name" -t "$REGISTRY/fake-image-$name:latest" .
done

echo "List images"
podman image ls "$REGISTRY/fake-image*"

echo "Generate bug container. Expected FROM uses locally generated images"
cat <<EOF > Containerfile.bug
ARG VERSION=latest
FROM $REGISTRY/fake-image-one:\${VERSION} as one
ARG VERSION=latest
FROM $REGISTRY/fake-image-two:\${VERSION} as two
ARG VERSION=latest
FROM $REGISTRY/fake-image-three:\${VERSION} as three

FROM debian:trixie-20251117
COPY --from=one /one /
COPY --from=two /two /
COPY --from=three /three /
RUN env|sort && cat /one /two
EOF

echo "Expect not to download anything"
podman build -f Containerfile.bug .

echo "List images finally"
podman image ls "$REGISTRY/fake-image*"
```

And here is my output:
```
Generate containers for multistage
```

Then changed locally and rebuilt (another uniq image id)

Tried to build container, all ids reverted back to 53f0b37b86c4 (images we pulled, local changes discarded).

To work around this issue until a proper fix is available, I suggest using `--pull=never`.
Thank you @Honny1

unfortunately this works only "on dev's machine", as image must be present. In CI or otherwise, test script in topic produces:
```
Generate containers for multistage
STEP 1/1: FROM debian:forky-20260202
Error: creating build container: debian:forky-20260202: image not known
Error: example.registry.local/bug/fake-image-one:latest: image not known
STEP 1/1: FROM debian:forky-20260202

List images
REPOSITORY  TAG         IMAGE ID    CREATED     SIZE
Change containers locally: add file generation
build, but not push
STEP 1/2: FROM debian:forky-20260202
Error: creating build container: debian:forky-20260202: image not known
build, but not push
STEP 1/2: FROM debian:forky-20260202
Error: creating build container: debian:forky-20260202: image not known
build, but not push
STEP 1/2: FROM debian:forky-20260202
Error: creating build container: debian:forky-20260202: image not known
List images
REPOSITORY  TAG         IMAGE ID    CREATED     SIZE
Generate bug container. Expected FROM uses locally generated images
Expect not to download anything
[1/4] STEP 1/2: FROM example.registry.local/bug/fake-image-one:latest AS one
Error: creating build container: example.registry.local/bug/fake-image-one:latest: image not known
List images finally
REPOSITORY  TAG         IMAGE ID    CREATED     SIZE

```
Yes, that is expected. You need to have the correct image in storage. This is a simple workaround that won't work in all use cases.

Actually, just someone want to know, my workaround is not use pattern `FROM xxx as YYY .... COPY --from=YYY`.

If I just use `COPY --from=example.registry.local/bug/fake-image-one:latest ...` podman behaves properly (takes local version)

I investigated the issue. The problem occurs during `multi-stage` builds because platforms are explicitly set to each stage, which forces the image to be pulled from the registry. Reference: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6104

Forcing a pull from the registry is caused by an inability to correctly identify the `platform` of a local image. Currently, it relies on data from the image config blob (`inspectInfo`), and many images don't set the variant field in their config. When `image.matchesPlatform` reads the config and finds an empty variant, `platform.Normalize` fills in a default value.

For example, for `ARM`, the default is `v7`. This leads to the following scenario:
- You pull a `linux/arm/v6` image.
- Its config blob has `architecture: "arm", variant: ""` (empty).
- `Normalize("", "arm", "")` -> `("linux", "arm", "v7")`.
- Now the image appears to be `linux/arm/v7`.
- When you ask "does this match `linux/arm/v7?`" -> yes (false positive).
- When you ask "does this match `linux/arm/v6`?" -> no (false negative, as it was actually v6).

The `variant` is simply not a reliable field in image configs. However, the manifest list is the authoritative source where we can get the correct `variant` for an image, as suggested in https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/82.

My idea is to fix the pulling logic when a custom platform is requested. We should force a pull only when the local image's platform doesn't match the requested platform. Specifically, we should verify the image match based on the manifest list.

Here is some pseudocode illustrating this approach:

```go
copySingleImageFromRegistry(imageName, pullPolicy, options):
    localImage = lookupLocalImage(imageName)
    
    if customPlatform AND pullPolicy not in (Always, Never):
        os, arch, variant = normalize(options)
        
        if localImage == nil OR NOT localImageMatchesPlatform(localImage, ...):
            pullPolicy = "newer"   // force registry check 
            // or 
            localImage = nil
        // else: keep original pullPolicy (e.g. "missing") → use local image
    
    ... continue with pullPolicy ...


localImageMatchesPlatform(image, os, arch, variant, requestedVariant):
    match, err = matchViaStoredManifestList(image, os, arch, variant)
    if err == nil:
        return match
    
    if NOT matchViaConfig(image, os, arch, variant):
        return false
    
    if requestedVariant == "":
        return true 
    
    registryDigest = getDigestFromRegistry(imageName, os, arch, variant)
    return image.hasDigest(registryDigest)


matchViaStoredManifestList(image, os, arch, variant):
    for each bigdata in image.BigData:
        if bigdata is a multi-arch manifest list:
            chosenDigest = list.ChooseInstance(os, arch, variant)
            return image.hasDigest(chosenDigest)
    return error("no stored manifest list")
```

I am not sure if making a round trip to the repository for the manifest makes sense. Also, I'm unsure if this improved platform matching should be integrated directly into the image lookup process to be more precise. WDYT? @nalind @mtrmac 

Related:
- https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/82
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17063
- https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1299
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23055
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10682
- https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6104
- https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4520
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28038#issuecomment-3881369078

This will need to resolve names to IDs as early as possible to minimize confusion when builds are being run for multiple target platforms at the same time.
The bit that checks if the requested variant value is empty could be looking at an image with a non-empty value, and I'm not sure if we were ever happy about how we handle those.
Lookups _could_ also be checking if there are any images with different image IDs but which match the digest that `ChooseInstance()` returns, since the name may have been moved from an image ID that we want to one for a different platform that was pulled more recently.
Does the logic which checks if an image is "newer" compare timestamps?  IIRC the error case here was one where a locally-built image was being overwritten by an older one from a registry.

I agree that starting with a manifest list, doing some version of `ChooseInstance` on that to get exactly one wanted manifest digest, and then seeing if we have _that_ image locally, is conceptually the right way to do local lookups. (Then there’s https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/82#issuecomment-3227121443 — should we do that even when the user does not explicitly specify a platform?! That might be a breaking change.)

Nalin is right that we might find the manifest list in one image (c/storage image ID) and the per-platform instance in a different image (image ID), if the last pull of a tagged image was for a different platform but we did pull the wanted instance previously.

---

If we do that lookup using a manifest list, I’m not much of a fan of doing _also_ a lookup using a config — it makes the behavior harder to understand — but maybe we need that for local multi-platform builds (which don’t create manifest lists by default, I think).

I hope Buildah fills in the variant field in the config exactly the same as we would fill in the manifest list, so at least in _that_ case there is no semantics difference.

Would we consider, in the config matching, to require _exact_ variant matches? That way, in theory local builds could work correctly (if we build a previous stage for v6 and look for a v6 image, fine), but we would avoid the corner cases where a manifest list contains a v6 and v7, we have v7 locally, the user asks for v6, and we find the v7 and the platform “matches” (but if we pulled from a registry, we would get a v6, not v7). (I think we _are_ looking for exact matches now — _except_ for defaulting the `variant` field — but I just wanted to make the discussion of “matches exactly” vs. “is compatible with” explicit.)

---

I don’t think the `getDigestFromRegistry` part is _truly_ necessary — we can “just” issue a pull and it should complete quickly if it finds the image existing locally. (If it does not complete quickly, I’d prefer optimizing that, not adding a special case restricted to non-default platform pulls.)

---

This is all even more complex due to deduplication, though — a single c/storage image ID may refer to a deduplicated image from several different registries/repos, and so it can also contain a set of _manifest lists_ from several different registries/repos. Those would _hopefully_ not be semantically different (although that _can_ happen in principle), but some workflows explicitly tag and upload per-platform images individually. So we might find an amd64 image of `baseImage:all-architectures` deduplicated with `baseImage:amd64`, with two manifest lists, one amd64-only, one with many platforms. And right now we have no way to tell which one is “right” for the users’ query.

I can _imagine_ a hack (don’t do anything and force a pull if there is no manifest list, or more than one manifest list), but that’s unsatisfactory.

---

Conceptually, I think c/storage _should_ be maintaining a mapping (repo:tag → repo:digest (referring to the top-level manifest list if we saw it)) for each tag we pull. We’ve needed that for years, to get correct “repoTags” values, and never got around to building it.

That would make it trivial to determine the architecture for the last-pulled image which still holds the tag in c/storage; but if the user does `for arch in a1 a2 a3; do podman pull --arch=$arch $repo:$tag`, I’m not exactly sure how that would work. I _think_ we would keep the repo:tag → repo:digest records for the older images around, even if we move the actual repo:tag pointer, but that would need more thought or a prototype.

Thanks for the feedback, @nalind and @mtrmac. I'll start with a prototype PR in `containers/podman` (modifying the vendor copy of `containers/common` to test the approach) before doing the proper upstream PR in `containers/common`, since this is not a simple change.
JFYI: in 5.8.1 still reproduced.

Also ran into it in 5.8.2 as well.  The bug itself is fairly problematic, not only effecting multi-stage builds, but also having the image you just built locally, sneakily replaced by the remote image when building other dependent images and doing local development.  Eagerly looking for a fix.
Sorry, I got pulled into higher-priority tasks. I will try to at least prototype a solution.
